design_philosophy.core_loop: Collect all gold pieces in a level while avoiding guard enemies, then reach the exit. Dig holes to trap guards or create traversal paths. Master counterintuitive enemy AI patterns.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource collection under pursuit—guards hunt the player while gold must be gathered. Enemy AI is deliberately unpredictable, requiring players to develop intuition for guard movement rather than memorize patterns.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Outthinking and outmaneuvering adversaries through spatial problem-solving; using environment manipulation (hole-digging) as both offensive and defensive tool.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Lode Runner is opinionated about teaching players emergent puzzle-solving rather than reflexive skill. It rejects the jump mechanic entirely. It embraces counterintuitive enemy behavior as a learning curve rather than a bug—guards don't chase optimally, forcing players to develop intuition. The level editor and 150-level progression suggest belief in player creativity and incremental challenge.

difficulty.lives_system: Player starts with 5 lives. Each level completion awards 1 extra life. Catching by a guard costs 1 life and restarts the current level.
difficulty.checkpointing: Per-level checkpointing. No mid-level checkpoints; level restart on guard contact.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Hidden level select available via Select button + A/B/Start allows players to skip ahead or practice specific levels. Speed adjustment via Select+A (up to 50x) allows manipulation of game velocity, granting players control over pacing and reaction time demands.

progression.unlock_mechanism: 150 levels with progressive difficulty. Levels unlock sequentially upon completion. Level select cheat allows arbitrary access.
replayability_hooks: 150 distinct level designs encourage mastery and speedrunning
Level editor presence (mentioned as series feature) suggests custom content creation
Adjustable game speed allows different difficulty self-tuning
Guard AI unpredictability means solutions vary in execution even with memorized patterns

world_building_techniques: Stick figure protagonist suggests abstraction over narrative
Multi-story brick platform aesthetic establishes consistent environment
Gold collection framing provides implicit context without exposition

danger_signals: Guards respawn at random locations upon hole-entrapment—unpredictable threat redeployment
Guards carrying gold can be weaponized or blocked—player-enemy resource contention
Hole-digging creates environmental hazards that affect both player and guards—mutual vulnerability
environmental_storytelling: Multi-story platforms suggest industrial/mining setting with vertical hierarchy
Gold distribution across levels implies puzzle-based treasure hunt
Trapdoors and bedrock introduce environmental constraints that modify solution space per level

sources_used: Wikipedia: Lode Runner (gameplay mechanics, enemy AI, difficulty systems, level progression)
The Cutting Room Floor: Lode Runner (NES) (level select cheat, speed adjustment, regional differences)
